title: Closed form of A: (Ax)_i = (1/i)∫_0^1 (1-u^i)x(u)du via Fubini

comments.author: human
comments.body:
**Sub-lemma (Step 1).** Prove that for all $x\in L^2(0,1)$,
$$(Ax)_i=\int_0^1 t^{i-1}\Big(\int_0^t x(u)\,du\Big)dt=\frac{1}{i}\int_0^1(1-u^i)\,x(u)\,du,$$
so that $A$ acts as $(Ax)_i=\langle a_i,x\rangle_{L^2}$ with rows $a_i(u)=(1-u^i)/i$.

**What must be shown.** Justify the Fubini–Tonelli interchange
$$\int_0^1 t^{i-1}\!\int_0^t x(u)\,du\,dt=\int_0^1\Big(\int_u^1 t^{i-1}\,dt\Big)x(u)\,du,$$
using $x\in L^2(0,1)\subset L^1(0,1)$ and the bounded measurable kernel $t^{i-1}\mathbf 1_{\{u\le t\}}$ on $(0,1)^2$, then evaluate $\int_u^1 t^{i-1}dt=(1-u^i)/i$.

**Why it matters.** This reduction is the foundation: it converts the abstract composition into an explicit family of $L^2$ rows whose Gram matrix
$$G_{ij}=\langle a_i,a_j\rangle=\frac{1}{ij}\Big(1-\tfrac1{i+1}-\tfrac1{j+1}+\tfrac1{i+j+1}\Big)$$
satisfies $\sigma_i(A)=\sqrt{\lambda_i(G)}$ (verified numerically). Low difficulty but mandatory for a gap-free proof.
